target.title: test(shared): add unit tests for account enabled guard
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The `account-enabled.ts` module provides a runtime guard for account enabled state, handling the boundary where `enabled` may be undefined, explicitly false, null, or the account may be a primitive value. Without tests, the default-to-enabled behavior could silently break.

## Why This Change Was Made

Add unit tests for `isAccountEnabled` covering the full boundary: true/false/undefined/null enabled values, missing field, null/undefined account, string account, and empty object.

## User Impact

Account enabled guard now has comprehensive test coverage, reducing regression risk.

## Evidence

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds direct coverage for existing helper behavior.

Direct behavior probe with real function calls:

```text
$ node --import tsx -e "import('./src/shared/account-enabled.js').then(({isAccountEnabled})=>console.log(JSON.stringify([{i:{enabled:true},r:isAccountEnabled({enabled:true}),e:true},{i:{enabled:false},r:isAccountEnabled({enabled:false}),e:false},{i:null,r:isAccountEnabled(null),e:true},{i:{},r:isAccountEnabled({}),e:true}])))"
[{"i":{"enabled":true},"r":true,"e":true},{"i":{"enabled":false},"r":false,"e":false},{"i":null,"r":true,"e":true},{"i":{},"r":true,"e":true}]
```

Targeted test:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/shared/account-enabled.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  9 passed (9)
  Duration  372ms
```

Formatting:

```text
$ npx oxfmt --check src/shared/account-enabled.ts src/shared/account-enabled.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.
```

Whitespace:

```text
$ git diff --check
```
